WARNING: These test points are for A2338 M2 (820-02773) ONLY. For other boards, use that board's diagnostic files.
title: 5V DCPS Triage — No Power / Dead Board
description: Step-by-step diagnostic for A2338 M2 MacBook Pro 13" presenting with no power. Uses 5V DCPS (DC Power Supply) on USB-C port. Board has NO MagSafe — USB-C only.

steps.name: Check PPVBUS_USBC0 (USB-C VBUS Port 0)
steps.action: Connect DCPS via USB-C to left rear port (debug port). Measure VBUS at CF400/CF401 decoupling caps.

if_missing.cause: USB-C connector damage, open trace, or CD3217 (UF400) MOSFET failure
checks: Inspect USB-C connector for bent/broken pins
Check continuity from USB-C VBUS pins to CF400
Check CC lines (USBC0_CC1, USBC0_CC2) for shorts to ground
Try left front port (UF500 / ATC1) instead

steps.name: Check PPBUS_AON
steps.action: Measure PPBUS_AON at PBUS capacitors (page 27 shows C5800-C5807 PBUS caps for SPF compliance).
steps.expected: Should rise toward battery voltage (~4-5V from DCPS at 5V, or 12.6V if battery connected)

if_missing.cause: Charger IC U5200 fault, or input not reaching charger
checks: Check SYS_DETECT at TP5100/TP5101 (page 24) — should be high when battery flex connected
Check CHGR_AUX_OK signal (page 26) — should be pulled up
Check for short on PPBUS_AON (diode mode on PBUS caps)
Check PPCON_ACNSW — is VBUS reaching charger input?
Measure current draw: >2A at 5V = short on PPBUS_AON

steps.name: Check PP3V8_AON
steps.action: Measure PP3V8_AON at decoupling capacitors on output side of SVR AON.
steps.ic: SVR AON controller (36A ICC MAX, pages 28-29)

if_missing.cause: SVR AON controller failure, enable not asserted, or downstream short pulling rail down
checks: Check PPVAON_PWR_EN — is the enable asserted? (U5349 delay circuit, page 26)
Check PPVAON_FAULT_L — is fault signal active low? (page 28)
Diode mode check PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN caps — common short target
Check U5910 current sense amplifier output (page 30)
If PP3V8_AON is shorted: disconnect WiFi module (PP3V8_AON_WLBT) to isolate

steps.name: Check MPMU AON LDOs
steps.action: Measure MPMU always-on LDO outputs. These should come up immediately after PP3V8_AON.

if_missing.cause: MPMU U8100 failure, or short on AON LDO output
checks: Diode mode each rail for shorts
If PP1V8_AON shorted — check for short to ground on PP1V8_AON_MPMU net
If all AON LDOs missing — U8100 itself is likely dead
Thermal camera to find hot component pulling rail down

steps.name: Check PP5V_S2
steps.action: Measure PP5V_S2 at output capacitors of UC300.

if_missing.cause: UC300 failure, enable not asserted, or downstream short
checks: Check P5VS2_PWR_EN — should be asserted after PMU startup (MPMU GPIO, page 40)
Check P5VS2TPS_PGOOD output (page 43)
Diode mode PP5V_S2 output for short
Check 5VS2_EN turn off delay (DC401/RC401, page 44)

steps.name: Check PP3V3_S2
steps.action: Measure PP3V3_S2 at output capacitors of UC710.

if_missing.cause: UC710 failure, enable not asserted, or downstream short
checks: Check P3V3S2_PWR_EN — should be asserted after PMU startup
Check P3V3S2_PGOOD output (page 45)
Diode mode PP3V3_S2 output for short
Check PP3V3_S2 discharge circuit (QC410/QC419, page 44)
